channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Former Wallabies coach handed ban and pay cut for abusing officials
item.description: Eddie Jones has been suspended from coaching Japan during its rugby union Test match against Italy in July after "incidences of verbal abuse" during a recent tour of Australia.
item.dc:creator: Michael Doyle

media:group.media:description: Eddie Jones has been suspended by the Japan Rugby Football Union.

item.title: Team smashed by more than 1,100 points in four games forfeits season
item.description: A country Victorian football team that was beaten by a collective 1,101 points in four matches forfeits the remainder of the senior season.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Miles

media:group.media:description: The Dennington Bulldogs were beaten by more than 300 points during their round four match with Russells Creek.

item.title: Alex Murdaugh murder convictions overturned
item.description: An infamous case will be retried after a US court ruled in favour of the South Carolina father.

media:group.media:description: Alex Murdaugh during a hearing on the motion for a retrial in January 2024.

item.title: Live: Cost of war on agenda as Trump meets Xi, UAE denies Netanyahu's secret visit
item.description: Donald Trump is expected to ask for China's help to resolve the costly and unpopular conflict with Iran, but analysts say he is unlikely to get the support he wants. Follow live.

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump arrives in Beijing.

item.title: Breaking: Almost 900 workers evacuated after chemical spill at meat processing plant
item.description: Five people were hospitalised after the liquid ammonia spill occurred just after midnight at a meat processing facility in Ipswich.

media:group.media:description: Emergency crews respond to a chemical spill at a meat processing plant in Ipswich, May 14 2026.

item.title: Lithium giant's free car wash proposal 'ironic' for dust-covered town
item.description: Residents plagued by dust from Australia's oldest and largest lithium mine say a plan for a free car wash by the mine's owner misses the mark.
item.dc:creator: Neve Brissenden

media:group.media:description: Residents are concerned about dust from Talison Lithium's Greenbushes Mine.

item.title: Australia launches first America's Cup challenge in 25 years
item.description: Australia launches its first challenge for the America's Cup in a quarter of a century, entering a team for the 38th edition of the historic sailing event in Naples, Italy, next year.

media:group.media:description: New Zealand is the holder of the America's Cup.

item.title: SA government plans to remove 10-year fracking ban in state's South East
item.description: The South Australian government is planning to lift a ban on fracking in the state's South East amid reports of increasing risks to gas supply in southern parts of the nation.

media:group.media:description: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas says the government plans to introduce the bill to parliament next week.

item.title: Public memorial for James Valentine to be held in Sydney
item.description: Sydney Town Hall will host a memorial for the late radio presenter and musician, with limited spots available for members of the public.
item.dc:creator: Declan Bowring

media:group.media:description: Valentine was a musician before he had a 30-year career presenting at the ABC.

item.title: Runner takes on mountainous 231km to honour son's memory
item.description: Running hundreds of kilometres up and down the Larapinta Trail is a gruelling challenge most people will never attempt. But for Ben Mack, completing it would be a dream come true.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Ellis

media:group.media:description: Ben Mack is set to run 231km in the West Macs Sonder Monster race to honour his late son.

item.title: US Senate confirms Trump's pick Kevin Warsh to lead Federal Reserve
item.description: The US Senate has confirmed Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump's nominee to lead the Federal Reserve, bringing new leadership to the world's most powerful central bank at a fraught moment for the global economy.

media:group.media:description: Kevin Warsh is the former governor of the US Federal Reserve.

item.title: Child sex offender resigns as Melbourne Karen Buddhist group president
item.description: A convicted child sex offender who was told he was exempt from requiring a Working With Children Check has resigned as the president of a community organisation.
item.dc:creator: Nate Woodall

media:group.media:description: San Maung Saw Wah has resigned as president of the Melbourne Karen Buddhist Association.

item.title: Drug counsellor who helped supply ketamine to Matthew Perry jailed
item.description: The man who delivered Friends star Matthew Perry the doses of ketamine that killed him says he is haunted by the mistakes he made.

media:group.media:description: Erik Fleming has been sentenced to serve a two-year term in jail.

item.title: Teenager charged over home invasion linked to Melbourne arson attacks
item.description: Police say a shot was fired by intruders during an aggravated home invasion earlier this month, which is being linked to a series of arson attacks on licensed venues in Melbourne.

media:group.media:description: Victoria Police say a shot was fired during the home invasion in Pakenham.

item.title: Live: Coalition wants to bar non-citizens from accessing NDIS, welfare payments
item.description: The Coalition has proposed barring new migrants from accessing welfare benefits until they are Australian citizens. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Boscaini

media:group.media:description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor. Taken 14/5/2026. ABC News: Matt Roberts

item.title: New virus to control surging rabbit population at least 10 years away
item.description: Feral rabbits are causing damage on farms and in towns, but biological control research has been delayed by funding woes.
item.dc:creator: Annie Brown

media:group.media:description: Joanne Sirl says feral rabbit holes in Bethanga's sports reserve are a safety hazard.

item.title: Virtual college expands HSC course offering to all NSW students
item.description: Students in Sydney, Newcastle and Wollongong will be able to enrol in a litany of academically challenging HSC subjects via an online school.
item.dc:creator: Chantelle Al-Khouri

media:group.media:description: An online college will expand its HSC course offerings to students across all NSW schools, giving them a wider range of subject choice.

item.title: Live: Warsh confirmed as US Fed chair, ASX to open slightly down
item.description: The Senate has confirmed Donald Trump's pick for the US Federal Reserve. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.
item.dc:creator: Alison Branley

media:group.media:description: The ASX is set to continue a recent weak run with three global central banks set to raise interest rates this week.

item.title: Residents fear houses could fall into ocean and nothing is being done
item.description: Residents living on an idyllic stretch of the NSW far south coast are demanding action to protect their homes from erosion.
item.dc:creator: Jacinta Counihan

media:group.media:description: The dune that protects houses from the ocean swell at Surfside is being eroded.

item.title: CIT chief told she could be fired for disclosing misconduct probe
item.description: Margot McNeill did not tell the Canberra Institute of Technology board she was under investigation for misconduct when she was being recruited.
item.dc:creator: Monte Bovill

media:group.media:description: Margot McNeill was appointed to the position of CIT chief executive in June last year.

item.title: Voss exit shuts door on Carlton's premiership era that never was
item.description: Michael Voss leaves Carlton having taken the club to places it has rarely been in a quarter of a century, but still adrift from where it ultimately wants to be.
item.dc:creator: Chris De Silva

media:group.media:description: Michael Voss's four-year tenure as Carlton coach saw some incredible highs but a few too many lows.

item.title: Swans move annual Pride Game away from St Kilda after Collard case
item.description: The fallout continues from the Lance Collard AFL tribunal case, with St Kilda cut from the annual Pride Game against Sydney.

media:group.media:description: Sydney had played St Kilda in their annual Pride Game since 2016.

item.title: Gun shop owners grapple with uncertainty amid firearm reform
item.description: A lack of clarity on the South Australian government's position on gun control reform has caused a downturn in revenue for retailers.
item.dc:creator: Jenae Madden

media:group.media:description: Greg Sparrow owns Fisher Firearms, supplying guns to buyers and retailers across South Australia.

item.title: After long absence, Tasmania's short-stay figures just got released
item.description: Almost 500 dwellings in Tasmania have been converted to short-stay accommodation in just over a year. It's the first time short-stay data has been updated since the end of 2024.
item.dc:creator: Clancy Balen

media:group.media:description: Woman opens a door with a key, April 2018.

item.title: 'Won’t be good returns': Landlords divided on property investment after budget
item.description: Housing policy changes announced in the federal budget could make it easier for some to buy homes, but others are planning to sell before they lose too much.
item.dc:creator: Tri Ardhya, Jason Fang, and Natasya Salim

media:group.media:description: Rosa Dhayan lives 90km from her university campus because it is one of the only areas where she can afford to rent.
